items.title: RBI imposes monetary penalty on The Chikhli Urban Co-operative Bank Limited, Chikhli, Maharashtra
items.summary: The Reserve Bank of India (RBl) has, by an order dated July 8, 2026, imposed a monetary penalty of ₹13 lakh (Rupees Thirteen Lakh only) on The Chikhli Urban Co-operative Bank Limited, Chikhli, Maharashtra (the bank) for contravention of the provisions of Section 26A read with Section 56 of the Banking Regulation Act, 1949 (BR Act) and non-compliance with certain directions issued by RBI on ‘Finance for Housing Schemes – UCBs’, and ‘Know Your Customer (KYC)’. This penalty has been imposed in exercise of powers conferred on RBI, under the provisions of Section 47A(1)(c) read with Sections 46(4)(

items.title: RBI imposes monetary penalty on The Nawada Central Co-operative Bank Limited, Bihar
items.summary: The Reserve Bank of India (RBI) has, by an order dated July 02, 2026, imposed a monetary penalty of ₹50,000/- (Rupees Fifty Thousand only) on The Nawada Central Co-operative Bank Limited, Bihar (the bank) for non-compliance with certain directions issued by RBI on ‘Know Your Customer (KYC)’. This penalty has been imposed in exercise of powers conferred on RBI under the provisions of Section 47A(1)(c) read with Sections 46(4)(i) and 56 of the Banking Regulation Act, 1949. The statutory inspection of the bank was conducted by National Bank for Agriculture and Rural Development (NABARD) with refe

items.title: RBI imposes monetary penalty on The Sambalpur District Co-operative Central Bank Limited, Odisha
items.summary: The Reserve Bank of India (RBI) has, by an order dated July 02, 2026, imposed a monetary penalty of ₹8 lakh (Rupees Eight Lakh only) on The Sambalpur District Co-operative Central Bank Limited, Odisha (the bank) for contravention of the provisions of Section 26A read with Section 56 of the Banking Regulation Act, 1949 (BR Act) and non-compliance with certain directions issued by RBI on ‘Know Your Customer (KYC)’. This penalty has been imposed in exercise of powers conferred on RBI under the provisions of Section 47A(1)(c) read with Sections 46(4)(i) and 56 of the BR Act. The statutory inspecti

items.title: RBI imposes monetary penalty on Ashok Sahakari Bank Ltd., Ahmednagar, Maharashtra
items.summary: The Reserve Bank of India (RBI) has, by an order dated June 29, 2026, imposed a monetary penalty of ₹10 lakh (Rupees Ten Lakh only) on Ashok Sahakari Bank Ltd., Ahmednagar, Maharashtra (the bank) for non-compliance with provisions of Section 20(1) of the Banking Regulation Act, 1949 (BR Act). This penalty has been imposed in exercise of powers conferred on RBI under the provisions of Section 47A(1)(c) read with Sections 46(4)(i) and 56 of the Banking Regulation Act, 1949. The statutory inspection of the bank was conducted by RBI with reference to its financial position as on March 31, 2025. Ba

items.title: RBI imposes monetary penalty on Surat People’s Co-operative Bank Ltd., Surat
items.summary: The Reserve Bank of India (RBI) has, by an order dated July 10, 2026, imposed a monetary penalty of ₹13.30 lakh (Rupees Thirteen lakh and thirty thousand only) on Surat People’s Co-operative Bank Ltd., Surat (the bank), for non-compliance with certain directions issued by RBI on ‘Board of Directors - UCBs’. This penalty has been imposed in exercise of powers conferred on RBI under the provisions of Section 47A(1)(c) read with Sections 46(4)(i) and 56 of the Banking Regulation Act, 1949. The statutory inspection of the bank was conducted by RBI with reference to its financial position as on Mar

items.title: Reserve Bank convenes Third Annual Conference of Internal Ombudsmen
items.summary: The Reserve Bank of India (RBI) organised the third annual conference of Internal Ombudsmen (IO) 1 on July 13, 2026, in Mumbai. The Conference brought together IOs from across a representative spectrum of regulated entities including banks, non-banking financial companies (NBFCs), credit information companies (CICs) and other regulated entities, along with the Managing Directors and Chief Executive Officers, Executive Directors in charge of customer service, Principal Nodal Officers as well as RBI Ombudsmen and senior officials of the Reserve Bank. In his keynote address, Shri Swaminathan J.,

items.title: Premature redemption under Sovereign Gold Bond (SGB) Scheme - Redemption Price for premature redemption of SGB 2020-21 Series-IV due on July 14, 2026
items.summary: In terms of GOI notification F.No.4(4)-B(W&M)/2020 dated April 13, 2020 (SGB 2020-21 Series-IV-Issue date July 14, 2020) on Sovereign Gold Bond Scheme, premature redemption of Gold Bond may be permitted after fifth year from the date of issue of such Gold Bond on the date on which interest is payable. Accordingly, the next due date of premature redemption of the above tranche shall be on July 14, 2026. 2. Further, the redemption price of SGB shall be based on simple average of closing price of gold of 999 purity of previous three business days from the date of redemption, as published by the I

items.title: Trump proposes 20% toll on cargo through Strait of Hormuz; restarts Iran blockade
items.summary: The Strait of Hormuz is a major oil-shipping route and the epicenter of the U.S. and Israel's ongoing war with Iran.

items.title: 21% YoY rise in engineering exports in June shows sector's resilience amid global challenges: EEPC India Chairman Pankaj Chadha
items.summary: Engineering exports rose nearly twenty-one percent year-on-year in June. This growth reached eleven point four eight billion dollars, reflecting sector resilience. Cumulative exports for the first quarter also showed an eighteen percent increase. Free trade agreements and market diversification contributed to this strong performance.

items.title: Biocon to see block deals, as Viatris plans to exit
items.summary: Floor price has been fixed as ₹378.50 a share, a discount of 7.9 per cent to current price

items.title: Small biz outstanding grows 13% in FY26, asset quality improves: Report
items.summary: Small business credit exposure grew by 13.4 percent to Rs 49.2 lakh crore by March 2026. Asset quality improved, with overdue balances now at four percent. Sole proprietorship concerns led this significant portfolio expansion. State-run banks showed accelerated enterprise term loan portfolio growth. Manufacturing sector received thirty-one percent of these small business loans.

items.title: SK Hynix US-listed shares slip nearly 8% as Nasdaq debut euphoria cools
items.summary: SK Hynix’s US-listed shares fell nearly 8% after a strong Nasdaq debut, as investors booked profits in AI-linked chip stocks. The decline triggered broader weakness across global semiconductor stocks, with sharp losses in Seoul and the US, reflecting growing concerns over valuations and sustainability of AI-driven demand.

items.title: Ahead of IPO, Millworks Tech raises nearly Rs 44 cr from anchor investors
items.summary: Millworks Technologies has successfully garnered Rs 44 crore from its anchor investors ahead of its public offering set to commence on July fourteenth. This large-scale IPO, amounting to Rs 160.33 crore, will close on July sixteenth. Renowned for its precision components across multiple sectors, the firm plans to utilize the raised funds to bolster capital expenditures and meet working capital demands.

items.title: SBI MF IPO to open on Tuesday, fund house hopes issue will bring investors back
items.summary: Price band fixed ₹545–574 per share with a face value of Re 1

items.title: HDFC Bank's total workforce witnesses reduction of 3,343 employees in FY26
items.summary: HDFC Bank reduced its workforce by 3,343 employees during the last fiscal year. The bank's total balance sheet expanded by twelve percent to over forty-three trillion rupees. Employee attrition stood at twenty-three percent, while senior management roles saw an increase.

items.title: India's exports to West Asia region evened out in June: Commerce Secretary
items.summary: India's exports to West Asia grew 7.29 percent to USD 5 billion in June. Shipments to the UAE increased while those to Saudi Arabia saw a decline. Imports from the UAE and Oman registered positive growth during this period. Indian exporters are utilizing Omani ports to facilitate regional trade. The US-Iran conflict had previously impacted international shipping routes.

items.title: Area sown to kharif crops remains 16% on weak monsoon; paddy, pulses acreage down: Agriculture Ministry
items.summary: Kharif crop sowing is 16 percent lower this season. This delay is attributed to a weak monsoon and El Nino effects. Area under pulses and coarse cereals has significantly declined. Oilseeds and cotton coverage also shows a notable decrease. Sugarcane and jute acreage, however, have seen slight increases.

items.title: Supreme Court refuses to stay SAT relief for Bombay Dyeing, Wadias
items.summary: Court says SAT's 2:1 split verdict will not serve as a precedent in similar cases

items.title: Small-cap stock under ₹50 hits upper circuit after this AI update
items.summary: Blue Cloud Softech Solutions shares closed 5% higher at ₹21.70 after signing a five-year Master Services Agreement with SpaceX International.

items.title: Aditya Birla Group acquires Shell's India renewables energy arm, Sprng Energy, for $1.8bn
items.summary: Aditya Birla Group announced acquiring Sprng Energy for $1.8 billion. This significant deal expands the group's renewable energy portfolio substantially. The acquisition includes utility-scale solar, wind, and hybrid assets across India. This transaction positions Aditya Birla Renewables among the nation's largest developers. Regulatory approvals are now pending for the completion of this major acquisition.

items.title: Mylan launches block deal to sell minority stake in Biocon for up to $363 million
items.summary: The Viatris arm looks to offload 92 million equity shares, marking a financial exit following a series of biosimilar asset integrations.

items.title: India's exports to US dip 1.21% in June, imports rise 33.86%
items.summary: India's exports to the US saw a slight dip in June, while imports increased significantly. Merchandise exports declined marginally during April-June, with imports showing a substantial rise. Trade with China experienced robust growth in both exports and imports during the period. Exports to Singapore and several other nations registered positive growth.

items.title: Laser Power & Infra IPO: Issue booked 41 times on Day 03; NII, QIB portions see strong demand
items.summary: Laser Power & Infra Ltd.'s IPO was oversubscribed 41 times, with institutional investors leading. The issue raised ₹742 crore, priced at ₹203-214 per share. The grey market premium suggests potential listing above the issue price, slated for July 16 after allotments on July 14.

items.title: Wall Street tech stocks fall after Asian chipmakers hammered
items.summary: Oil rises as US-Iran hostilities escalate and investors go into ‘risk-off’ mode

items.title: Global Markets: Shein eyes up to $3 billion Hong Kong IPO as soon as August, Bloomberg News reports
items.summary: Fast-fashion powerhouse Shein is gearing up for an initial public offering in Hong Kong, aiming to secure between two to three billion dollars in funds. The company's long-awaited entry into the public market has been greenlit by China's securities regulator. With a targeted valuation between forty and fifty billion dollars, this listing comes after previous unsuccessful attempts in various global markets.

items.title: UK strikes landmark trade deal with Switzerland for crucial services sector
items.summary: The British government estimates the free trade agreement to unlock £5.2 billion a year in exports to Switzerland over the coming years.

items.title: India-UK trade pact: CBIC issues guidelines on implementation of self-certification of origin declarations
items.summary: New guidelines for self-certification of origin declarations are now in effect. This change applies to the India-UK Comprehensive Economic and Trade Agreement starting July fifteenth. The agreement replaces traditional certificates with a self-declaration framework for origin verification. Customs authorities in both nations will manage the authentication process for imports. Origin declarations will remain valid for twelve months from their completion date.

items.title: Q1 Results Today Live: Bajaj Consumer Care Q1 profit rises, HCL Tech profit up 20%, ICICI Prudential AMC, Nuvoco Vistas Corporation, Khaitan Chemicals to announce Q1 results
items.summary: Q1 Results Today, July 13, 2026, Live Updates: Follow businessline's Q1 updates here

items.title: Truecaller row: TRAI seeks 'designated agency' status under IT Act; Meity examining it
items.summary: Telecom regulator TRAI wants to be an authorized agency under the IT Act. This status would allow TRAI to take action against call management applications. The IT Ministry is currently examining this request for regulatory jurisdiction. TRAI recently asserted that no app can block government and regulated entity calls. The Ministry is reviewing the matter, not specifically the Truecaller dispute.

items.title: Gold drops more than 1% as rising Middle East tensions add to rate-hike fears
items.summary: Gold prices saw a notable decline amidst rising tensions in the Middle East, driven by concerns over inflation linked to escalating oil prices. The prospect of an interest rate hike from the Federal Reserve in September is keeping traders on edge as they await crucial economic data releases this week. Prices for other precious metals also suffered losses on Monday.

items.title: Supreme Court upholds SEBI action against Kotak AMC in Essel case
items.summary: Court says compliance with mutual fund rules is ‘mandatory and non-negotiable’; imposes ₹50 lakh litigation costs on Kotak AMC and trustee

items.title: UP to get road projects worth Rs 5 lakh crore in next two years: RTH Minister Nitin Gadkari
items.summary: Union Minister Nitin Gadkari announced road projects worth Rs 5 lakh crore for Uttar Pradesh. New projects worth Rs 50,000-60,000 crore have also been approved for the state. Infrastructure development is considered the foundation for economic growth and investment attraction. The minister highlighted the accelerated development pace in Uttar Pradesh since 2014.

items.title: Mixed trend in gold ETFs over past 2 weeks, as investors keep a low profile
items.summary: Renewed tensions in West Asia due to US-Iran conflict forces gold to give up gains

items.title: IndiGo, Accor announce reciprocal loyalty programmes partnership
items.summary: IndiGo and Accor have announced a new loyalty partnership in India. Members can now convert reward points between IndiGo BluChip and ALL Accor. This collaboration connects air travel with rewarding hospitality experiences for customers. Travellers can seamlessly link flights and hotel stays across both networks. The partnership aims to create a more rewarding travel journey within India and beyond.

items.title: US stocks today: S&P 500, Nasdaq decline as Iran tensions jolt sentiment; chip stocks fall
items.summary: On Monday, stock markets dipped as rising oil prices intensified due to increasing tensions between the U.S. and Iran. This surge in oil costs exacerbated the ongoing decline in chip stocks, heavily weighing on the Nasdaq index. Investors are now tasked with weighing strong corporate earnings against escalating geopolitical uncertainties.

items.title: Broker’s call: India Nippon Elec (Buy)
items.summary: SBI Securities

items.title: Mylan to sell up to $363 million stake in India's Biocon, term sheet shows
items.summary: Mylan ​Inc plans to ‌sell ⁠up ⁠to 92 ​million shares in ​Indian drugmaker ​Biocon ⁠Ltd, worth ‌up ​to ​34.81 ⁠billion Indian rupees ($363 ​million), according to ​a term ‌sheet seen ​by ​Reuters.

items.title: FCNR inflows may reduce banks' reliance on CD market in Q2 FY27: Report
items.summary: Foreign currency deposits may reduce bank reliance on certificate of deposit issuances. Strong credit growth will continue to support certificate of deposit issuances in the near term. However, FCNR (B) deposit inflows will determine certificate of deposit volumes in the second quarter. Commercial paper issuances are expected to remain strong for NBFCs and corporates. Elevated refinancing needs will sustain primary market activity for commercial papers.

items.title: Jindal Steel CEO Gautam Malhotra resigns ahead of board meeting next week
items.summary: Gautam Malhotra, CEO of Jindal Steel, has resigned from his position. His resignation is effective from July 15, 2026, due to personal commitments. Malhotra expressed appreciation for the support received from colleagues and the board. The company's board will meet on July 24, 2026, to approve financial results. Jindal Steel has not yet announced a replacement for the chief executive.

items.title: Big banks poised to report booming revenue propelled by SpaceX IPO, Iran war volatility
items.summary: Big banks are set to report booming Q2 revenue as the SpaceX IPO, Iran war volatility and a rebound in commercial lending fuel Wall Street's "sweet spot."

items.title: Asian Paints hikes prices by 12% as West Asia conflict drives up costs
items.summary: Asian Paints has increased prices by approximately twelve percent to counter rising raw material costs. This significant price adjustment is linked to the ongoing Middle East conflict and its impact. Rivals Berger Paints India and Kansai Nerolac Paints also raised their prices earlier. JSW Dulux implemented a ten percent price increase for its products. Indian paint manufacturers are adjusting prices to offset higher crude-linked raw material expenses.

items.title: FM Nirmala Sitharaman calls for enhanced NRI outreach to sustain foreign currency mobilisation momentum
items.summary: Finance Minister Nirmala Sitharaman urged banks to enhance outreach to Non Resident Indians. Banks are offering attractive returns on foreign currency deposits and are seeing significant interest. Public sector banks are employing customized digital strategies to engage the NRI diaspora effectively. International Banking Units at GIFT City are being utilized for fund mobilization from multiple jurisdictions.

items.title: Broker’s call: Indian Bank (Buy)
items.summary: The bank may achieve the upper end of the prior guidance of 3.1-3.25 per cent. Incrementally, MCLR could impact margin negatively by 2 bps, ceteris paribus

items.title: Treasury yields are little changed as U.S.-Iran ceasefire strained; investors await inflation data
items.summary: Treasury yields were broadly flat on Monday after the ceasefire agreement between the U.S. and Iran came under increasing strain over the weekend.

items.title: BITS Pilani-backed sortmyprep raises USD 350k in pre-seed round
items.summary: Sortmyprep, an AI education platform, secured USD 350k in pre-seed funding. This investment will strengthen its AI infrastructure and expand curriculum coverage. The platform currently serves students in over fifteen countries globally. It aims to enhance learning outcomes and exam performance for students. Sortmyprep plans to broaden its reach into new educational boards.

items.title: BSNL unaudited operational revenue up by 10% to Rs 4,418 crore in Q1: Jyotiraditya Scindia
items.summary: State-run BSNL reported a ten percent revenue increase in the first quarter. Enterprise business and consumer mobility segments showed growth for the company. The consumer fixed access segment remained almost flat during this period. Total revenue from operations reached Rs 4,418 crores this year. Average revenue per user provisionally increased from Rs 100 to Rs 102.7.

items.title: HCL Tech Q1 Results: Board declares interim dividend of ₹12 per share. Details here
items.summary: HCL Technologies announced an interim dividend of ₹12 per share, marking the 94th consecutive quarter of payouts. The record date for shareholders is July 17, with the payout scheduled for July 27.

items.title: U.S. and Iran exchange strikes as Strait of Hormuz standoff escalates
items.summary: Iran responded to a fresh wave of strikes by U.S. forces by launching an attack on American military bases in several Gulf states.

items.title: Quote of the day by Irving Fisher: "The chief evils of speculation flow from the participation of the general public, who lack the special knowledge, and enter the market in a purely gambling spirit"
items.summary: Irving Fisher's timeless observation warns against public speculation in financial markets. Retail investors often treat markets like casinos, driven by emotion and trends. Knowledge and discipline are crucial for successful investing, not quick profits. Easy access to trading platforms democratizes investing but increases speculative risks. Informed decisions consistently reward market participants more than impulsive speculation.

items.title: 'Funflation' hits home: Why staying in isn't the cost-saver it used to be
items.summary: Following a wave of price hikes, at-home pastimes like streaming movies or playing video games are pinching pocketbooks.

items.title: JSW Infrastructure among 7 stocks that hit 52-week highs and rallied up to 30% in a month
items.summary: Seven BSE 200 stocks, including Paytm, Nykaa, Oracle Financial Services and JSW Infrastructure, hit fresh 52-week highs despite a subdued market. The milestone reflects strong investor confidence, sustained buying interest and continued upward momentum in these counters.

items.title: HCL Tech Q1 Results: Profit rises 20% YoY to Rs 4,624 crore; co declares Rs 12 dividend
items.summary: HCL Technologies Q1 results: IT services company HCL Tech reported a 20% year-on-year (YoY) growth in its consolidated net profit at Rs 4,624 crore in the first quarter of FY27. The same stood at Rs 3,843 crore in the last year quarter.

items.title: Dow Jones| Nasdaq | S&P 500 | US Stock Market Today | Live: S&P 500, Nasdaq decline as Iran tensions jolt sentiment; chip stocks fall
UP RERA directs real estate developers to charge prescribed GST rates
items.summary: UP RERA mandates developers adhere to official Goods and Services Tax rates. Homebuyers can claim refunds for any excess GST collected by promoters. The State Tax Department has established an online mechanism for these refund applications. If a project is cancelled after the credit note period, buyers can apply directly. Refunds are admissible for GST amounts exceeding one thousand rupees.

items.title: Puravankara records 28% growth in Q1 pre-sales, collections up 40%
items.summary: Realty developer Puravankara reported a 28% year-on-year rise in pre-sales to ₹1,439 crore in the April-June quarter, while collections grew 40% to ₹1,199 crore. Average price realisation increased 18% to ₹10,589 per sq ft, and the company delivered 745 homes during the quarter.

items.title: HCL Tech Q1 results: Profit jumps 20% YoY to ₹4,626 crore; dividend declared; key takeaways from June quarter earnings
items.summary: HCL Tech Q1 results: Profit jumped 20% YoY to ₹4,626 crore.  Sequentially, or quarter-on-quarter (QoQ), HCL Tech's profit increased by 3%. The company did not make any changes to its guidance for FY27.

items.title: Market wrap: TCS, HCL Tech among top gainers and losers on Nifty and Sensex today
items.summary: Sensex and Nifty recovered sharply from nearly 1% intraday losses to close marginally higher, led by IT stocks. Analysts cited strong FII inflows and technical support at 24,000, while warning that rising crude oil prices remain the biggest market risk.

items.title: Laser Power and Infra IPO fetches 38.94 times subscription on final day
items.summary: Strong QIB demand at 92.25 times drives overall subscription amid anchor investment of ₹223 crore

items.title: CIC directs petroleum ministry arm to share historical petrol, ethanol, profit data
items.summary: The Central Information Commission ordered the Petroleum Planning and Analysis Cell to release historical petrol production and import data. It also mandated the disclosure of ethanol procurement and blending figures, along with petroleum sector profits. The commission found that the cell had initially provided only partial information to an applicant. PPAC must now furnish revised responses and specific web links for ethanol blending notifications.

items.title: Who will replace Lindsey Graham in the Senate?
items.summary: South Carolina GOP Rep. Nancy Mace and Gov. Henry McMaster are among the first to be floated for the Senate seat vacated by Lindsey Graham.

items.title: SK Hynix shares plunge 15% to one-month low after blockbuster Nasdaq debut
items.summary: SK Hynix shares fell 15% on 13 July, marking a record decline. The drop, driven by profit-taking after Nasdaq debut, weighed on South Korea's Kospi, which fell 9%. Market volatility raises questions on AI-driven valuations and sustainable returns amid uncertainty in earnings season.

items.title: India, US have framework trade deal ready; signing awaits right time: Official
items.summary: India and the United States are progressing well on a trade agreement framework. This framework deal is ready and will be signed at the appropriate time. India seeks a comparative advantage on tariffs compared to competitor nations. Both sides are fully engaged in achieving a balanced and meaningful trade pact. Discussions also cover US investigations into forced labor and excess capacity concerns.

items.title: Flipkart deepens West Bengal investments, backs jobs and MSMEs
items.summary: Flipkart will deepen its long-term investments in West Bengal's infrastructure. The company operates its largest fulfillment center in Haringhata, West Bengal. This facility supports over 120,000 direct and indirect jobs across various functions. Flipkart's marketplace empowers over 66,000 sellers from West Bengal. The e-commerce giant remains committed to the state's growth potential.

items.title: Indian real estate attracts record institutional investments in January-June period at $4.1 billion: Vestian
items.summary: Institutional investments in India's real estate sector reached a record USD 4.1 billion. This significant rise occurred during the first half of the year. Better inflows came from both domestic and global investors. Commercial assets attracted the largest share of these investments. Investor confidence in the real estate ecosystem is growing.

items.title: The SpaceX IPO made history. One month on has it lost momentum?
items.summary: A month on from the firm's stock market debut, the reality of how SpaceX currently makes money has seemed to come into clearer focus.

items.title: Edelweiss arm wins NHAI's TOT 19 roads bundle for ₹2,259 crore
items.summary: EAAA Alternatives has won the rights to operate two NHAI toll road projects in Tamil Nadu for 20 years under the Toll, Operate and Transfer (TOT) programme. The 170-km Thanjavur-Trichy and Madurai-Tuticorin stretches were acquired for ₹2,259 crore.

items.title: Markets stage comeback amid global sell-off, IT earnings boost
items.summary: Nifty 50 closed at 24,211.65, up just 4.75 points or 0.02%, while the BSE Sensex ended at 77,616.40, gaining 47 points or 0.06%

items.title: SC asks Baba Kalyani and Sugandha Hiremath to settle disputes via mediation
items.summary: The Supreme Court appointed former Supreme Court judge Justice L Nageswara Rao as the mediator in the case and urged the two parties to make one last attempt to bury the hatchet amicably.

items.title: From AI access to AI literacy: What students really need to learn before graduation
items.summary: As AI becomes ubiquitous, using it well matters more than using it at all. ET Masterclass's student programmes AI Orientation, Academic Excellence, AI Mastery, and Young Entrepreneur build critical thinking, verification skills, and entrepreneurial fundamentals, preparing students not just to access AI, but to think with it.

items.title: ICICI Prudential AMC Q1 Results: Net profit rises 23% YoY to Rs 965 crore; revenue up 18%
items.summary: ICICI Prudential AMC Q1 Results: Revenue from operations, meanwhile, increased 18% YoY to Rs 1,564 crore.

items.title: Sensex today | Stock Market Highlights: Stock markets end flat as West Asia tensions, higher oil prices weigh on sentiment
items.summary: Sensex, Nifty, Share Prices Highlights: Benchmark indices Sensex and Nifty ended flat on Monday as a sharp rally in crude oil prices due to escalating tensions in West Asia weighed on overall investor sentiment.

items.title: Rajnath Singh, Nitin Gadkari inaugurate Lucknow-Kanpur Expressway in UP
items.summary: Union ministers and Uttar Pradesh Chief Minister inaugurated the Lucknow-Kanpur Expressway. This new six-lane access-controlled highway cost Rs 4,200 crore to build. The project is expected to cut travel time between the two cities to just 40 minutes. It features an Intelligent Traffic Management System for enhanced safety and efficiency. The expressway will boost regional development and create new employment opportunities.

items.title: Can SBI Funds Management’s Rs 9,813 crore mega IPO revive a dry stock market? CEO makes bold prediction
items.summary: SBI Funds Management expects its Rs 9,813 crore IPO to revive investor confidence and boost India's primary market. CEO Debasish Mishra said attractive pricing, strong governance and industry growth prospects could encourage fresh investments and pave the way for more quality listings.

items.title: ICICI Pru AMC Q1 results: Profit jumps 23% YoY to ₹964.63 crore, revenue rises 17.5%
items.summary: ICICI Pru AMC Q1 results: ICICI Prudential Asset Management Company reported a 23% year-on-year rise in its profit to ₹964.63 crore for Q1FY27. Revenue from operations rose by 17.55% YoY to ₹1,564.22 crore.

items.title: India's inflation accelerates to 4.38% in June, exceeding forecasts
items.summary: Inflation has risen for the eighth straight month in India, as food and energy prices rise due to the Iran war and deficient rainfall.

items.title: Oil pangs resurface for rupee, options market gauge signals bearish bias
items.summary: On Monday, the Indian rupee fell to its lowest level in over a month, driven down by rising oil prices amid escalating tensions between the US and Iran. Despite the currency's decline, traders observed that the central bank's potential intervention helped mitigate further losses. Additionally, India's merchandise trade deficit saw a notable increase in June, raising concerns about persistent inflation that might lead to interest rate hikes.

items.title: DMart shares down after Q1 earnings
items.summary: The company reported a 12.8% y-o-y rise in standalone profit after tax (PAT) to ₹936 crore for the quarter ended June 30, 2026

items.title: Bansal Family to invest Rs 10,000 crore in FY27 on construction, land acquisitions
items.summary: The Bansal Family plans a Rs 10,000 crore investment in FY27 for land and construction. Their real estate platforms hold over 3,000 acres of land with substantial future development potential. The group has delivered over 30.6 million sq. ft. and is developing more projects. They are North India's largest retail developer and lead in branded residences. This expansion includes ultra-luxury districts and partnerships with global brands.

items.title: Kalpataru posts 17% jump in collections, launches luxury Mumbai project in Q1 FY27
items.summary: The company’s total market capitalisation stands at approximately ₹5,864 crore, the stock trades at a trailing price-to-earnings multiple of 71.27, which NSE flags as exceeding 50 for four consecutive trailing quarters

items.title: India merchandise trade gap hits a 5-month high in June on oil bill surge amid war, exports rise 15.5%
items.summary: India’s merchandise exports rose 15.5% to $40.4 billion in June, but the imports grew even faster, widening the merchandise trade deficit to a five-year high of $30.4 billion. The deficit for the June quarter was $86.86 billion, up from $68.75 billion a year ago.

items.title: India's retail inflation breaches RBI’s midpoint target, rises to 4.38% in June
items.summary: The median estimate in a Mint poll of 18 economists had forecast retail inflation at 4.2% in June.

items.title: India's June retail inflation quickens to 4.38%, crosses RBI's 4% target after 17 months
items.summary: India's retail inflation reached 4.38 percent in June, exceeding the central bank's target. Elevated food and fuel prices contributed to this significant acceleration in price pressures. The Reserve Bank of India maintained its benchmark repo rate at 5.25 percent during its review. Policymakers are balancing inflation risks with the need to support economic growth. Concerns remain about global energy prices and an erratic monsoon season.

items.title: Rupee slips to one-month low of 95.62 per US dollar as oil pangs resurface
items.summary: Oil prices rose after ‌US and Iran traded strikes, with Tehran saying ​it had closed the vital Strait of Hormuz.

items.title: SpiceJet, Ajay Singh undertake to pay Rs 140 crore in KAL Airways dispute
items.summary: SpiceJet and Ajay Singh will pay Rs 140 crore to KAL Airways and Kalanithi Maran. The airline will deposit Rs 50 crore within forty-five days. The remaining Rs 90 crore will be paid within ninety days thereafter. The Delhi High Court will monitor this payment schedule closely. Hearing SpiceJet's challenge to the arbitral award is deferred until compliance.

items.title: India seeks to quash Adani airport's nicotine pouch court challenge citing illegality, health risk
items.summary: India's government argues Adani Group's airport sale of nicotine pouches violates drug laws. The government asserts these sales pose a serious public health risk to citizens. Adani Group is challenging the official finding that they broke the law. The case could set a precedent for regulating nicotine pouch sales at airports. The High Court in Mumbai is scheduled to hear the legal arguments soon.

items.title: Sensex rises 47 points, Nifty closes above 24,200 as market recovers all morning losses. What’s ahead?
items.summary: Indian benchmark indices erased steep intraday losses to end marginally higher on Monday, supported by a strong rally in IT stocks and continued foreign fund inflows despite escalating tensions between the US and Iran. Analysts said the market's near-term direction will hinge on crude oil prices, with Brent remaining below the key $90-a-barrel threshold.

items.title: DU UG admissions 2026-27: Tentative schedule, venues of physical trials for ECA quota announced
items.summary: Delhi University's undergraduate admissions will feature ECA trials beginning July sixteenth. Detailed schedules for individual candidates will be published by July fourteenth. Candidates outside Delhi should book travel and plan accommodation near venues. Trials for dance, debate, and music commence on July sixteenth. The academic session is scheduled to start on July twenty-eighth.

items.title: The Rs 200 meal is the new battleground for Swiggy, Zomato and other food delivery companies
items.summary: Food delivery companies are adapting strategies to attract everyday officegoers. Swiggy and Zomato offer value meals and targeted discounts to customers. Ownly and Rebel Foods explore different pricing and occasion-based models. These platforms aim to make ordering food a regular habit. Affordability and varied offerings are key to future growth.

items.title: SC seeks aviation rules in sealed cover amid concerns over airfares, passenger rights
items.summary: The Supreme Court has ordered the Centre to submit new aviation rules within two weeks. These rules are under the Bharatiya Vayuyan Adhiniyam, 2024, which is awaiting finalization. A petition seeks stronger regulation and safeguards against arbitrary airfare hikes by airlines. The court is considering the creation of an independent aviation regulator for passenger protection. Further hearing on this significant matter is scheduled for August 3.

items.title: Top Gainers & Losers on 13 July: Newgen Software, Kalyan Jewellers, TCS, Voltas, Paytm, Bajaj Auto among top gainers
items.summary: Indian benchmark indices closed largely unchanged on July 13, supported by strong buying in IT and auto stocks. The Nifty 50 settled at 24,211, while the Sensex ended at 77,564. The Nifty IT index was the top gainer, rising 3.59%.

items.title: SC says SAT order in Bombay Dyeing case not to be treated as precedent, but refuses stay
items.summary: The Supreme Court declined to halt a Securities Appellate Tribunal order concerning Bombay Dyeing. SEBI's plea challenging the tribunal's decision was issued notice by the court. The tribunal had previously overturned SEBI's regulatory directions against the company and its promoters. This ruling involved transactions between Bombay Dyeing and SCAL Services Limited.

items.title: Stock market today: Sensex, Nifty 50 end flat; IT stocks shine
items.summary: The Sensex ended 47 points, or 0.06%, higher at 77,616.40. The NSE counterpart Nifty 50 closed the day with a minor gain of 4 points, or 0.02%, at 24,211.

items.title: Actor Ranbir Kapoor buys land parcel in Pune’s Mulshi for Rs 16.42 crore
items.summary: Bollywood actor Ranbir Kapoor has acquired over twenty-five acres of land in Pune's Mulshi taluka. These four adjoining land parcels were purchased for more than sixteen crore rupees. The largest acquisition involved a ten-acre plot bought for over seven crore rupees. Kapoor also recently purchased land in Ayodhya for over three crore rupees. Celebrity real estate investments remain robust across various Indian markets.

items.title: Legal sector regulator in England and Wales has ‘lost its way’, review finds
items.summary: Report into Legal Services Board follows a number of high-profile law firm collapses in recent years

items.title: When the ducks are quacking, feed them
items.summary: Memory mania redux

items.title: 'False, baseless': Piyush Goyal pushes back on India-US trade deal claims
items.summary: Commerce Minister Piyush Goyal has dismissed reports that India rejected a quick trade deal with the US as "completely false, baseless and misleading." He said both countries remain committed to a balanced bilateral agreement, while Trade Secretary Rajesh Agarwal said negotiations are progressing well and India sees no challenges in the talks.

items.title: Delhi HC orders Meta to take down ‘pirated’ Zee content from Facebook
items.summary: The social media giant must remove specific infringing links following allegations of illegal monetization, though the court declined to block the accounts entirely at this stage.

items.title: Puravankara Q1 sales bookings up 28% to Rs 1,439 cr on higher volumes, price growth
items.summary: Puravankara has achieved an impressive twenty-eight percent growth in sales bookings in the first quarter, reflecting strong market performance. Additionally, customer fund collections experienced a remarkable forty percent year-on-year increase, reaching Rs 1,199 crore. The sales area expanded by nine percent to 1.36 million square feet, while the average price realization rose by eighteen percent to Rs 10,589 per square foot.

items.title: Bajaj Consumer Care Q1 Results: Posts record Q1 as stock hits 52-week high
items.summary: EBITDA jumps 101.2% to ₹84.4 crore; margin expands to 24.7% from 15.8% YoY

items.title: Chipmaker TSMC sees 36% growth in sales, 68% surge in June revenues amid sustained global demand for AI hardware
items.summary: The Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing Company (TSMC) today reported 36% rise in quarterly sales and 68% in June revenues amid sustained global demand for artificial intelligence (AI) hardware.

items.title: LTM shares jump over 9% in a week; brokerages split as Q1 growth signals improve
items.summary: The stock has gained 5.81 per cent over the past month and 9.13 per cent over the past week, though it remains down 33.44 per cent year-to-date and 21.86 per cent over one year

items.title: Global Market: Eurozone bond yields climb as Middle East tensions rekindle inflation concerns
items.summary: Eurozone government bond yields climbed on Monday due to escalating US-Iran tensions. Oil prices surged significantly after Iran announced a closure of the Strait of Hormuz. This renewed conflict has increased concerns over global inflation and economic stability.

items.title: Goldman Sachs sees Nifty at 26,500 by June 2027 on return of foreign flows and domestic recovery
items.summary: Goldman Sachs expects a rotation in market leadership from growth to value stocks and from mid-cap companies to large-caps

items.title: SBI Funds IPO: Can its private-markets bet close the profitability gap?
items.summary: SBI Funds Management's IPO highlights a key question for investors: why India's biggest fund house earns less from its massive scale—and whether private credit can change that.

items.title: Sebi tightens ethics rules for current, former employees
items.summary: India's Securities and Exchange Board (SEBI) has put in place a two-year cooling-off period for past officials. The new regulations broaden investment restrictions to include the family members of employees. Notably, SEBI personnel are now required to step away from specific issues that involve personal affiliations and must report any future job discussions within a thirty-day timeframe. These changes take effect starting Monday.

items.title: Fino Payments Bank shares hit 20% upper circuit after June business update
items.summary: Fino Payments Bank's shares hit a four-month high, gaining 20% after positive June business updates. The bank reported an 11% rise in deposits and increased loan referrals, signaling growth amid some declines in transaction throughput. The stock remains significantly below its 52-week high.

items.title: Bajaj Consumer Care Q1 Results: Net profit jumps 84% YoY to ₹70.75 crore
items.summary: Bajaj Consumer Care saw an 84% YoY net profit surge to ₹70.75 crore for Q1 FY27, driven by a 25% revenue rise to ₹341.57 crore. The company reported growth in its Almond Drops brand and strong recovery in international markets despite regional disruptions.

items.title: India's June trade deficit jumps 59% YoY to $30.43 bn
items.summary: India's merchandise trade deficit widened to $30.43 billion in June from $28.21 billion in May, even as goods exports rose 15.5% year-on-year to $40.41 billion. In the April-June quarter, overall goods exports increased about 15%, supported by higher petroleum shipments and a recovery in exports to Gulf countries.

items.title: India tells clean energy firms to lose grid connection or generate power
items.summary: India's energy regulator has directed renewable firms to surrender transmission rights or provide higher bank guarantees. This move aims to free up grid capacity for projects that are actively generating electricity. Companies can also transfer rights to group utilities that are generating but lack grid connectivity. The Central Electricity Regulatory Commission believes this will release significant connectivity for other developers.

items.title: Textile stock Aastha Spintex hits 5% upper circuit after this order book update
items.summary: Textile stock Aastha Spintex hit 5% upper circuit at ₹129.02 apiece in Monday's trading session. The textile stock has fallen over 5.47% since its stock market debut on 6 July 2026.

items.title: Forget the metros, India's solar boom has a new address
items.summary: Tier-II and tier-III cities are leading India's rooftop solar adoption significantly. The Pradhan Mantri Surya Ghar scheme is driving this widespread adoption across the nation. Over 36 lakh rooftop solar systems have been installed, benefiting many households. Gujarat, Maharashtra, and Uttar Pradesh are the leading states in solar installations. The focus is now shifting towards urban areas and low-income households.

items.title: Aditya Birla Group proposes Rs 12,000 crore Odisha alumina refinery expansion
items.summary: Aditya Birla Group plans a significant Rs 12,000 crore investment for its Odisha alumina refinery. This expansion will triple the refinery's production capacity to three million tonnes annually. The project aims to boost India's aluminium value chain and create substantial local employment opportunities. Hindalco Industries, the group's flagship, reported strong financial results for the recent fiscal year. This investment will support rising domestic demand and reinforce Odisha's industrial hub status.

items.title: 'Entities involved say not much lost': IT Secy on probe into Tata Electronics cyber leak linked to Apple
items.summary: An alleged data breach at Tata Electronics is under examination by the IT Ministry. Reports suggest sensitive iPhone 18 Pro model data was exposed on the dark web. The Indian Computer Emergency Response Team is also investigating the security incident. Both Tata Electronics and Apple are reportedly satisfied with no significant commercial losses. The IT Secretary confirmed the ongoing study of the situation.

items.title: LME copper prices fall as escalating US-Iran war renews inflation risks, interest rate hike fears
items.summary: Copper prices dropped due to escalating US-Iran war, with LME three-month copper down 0.64%. The geopolitical tensions increased energy prices and inflation fears, while a stronger dollar led to lower demand for commodities like copper.

items.title: RPG Life Sciences launches Naprosyn ES, a first-in-India Naproxen-Esomeprazole combination
items.summary: The product targets patients on long-term NSAID therapy who face elevated risk of gastrointestinal complications.

items.title: SpiceJet vs Maran: Airline to deposit ₹144.5 crore in tranches
items.summary: The development marks a turnaround for SpiceJet in the legal battle after the airline had earlier argued that it lacked the liquidity to make an immediate cash deposit and had sought permission to furnish a commercial property in Gurugram as security

items.title: India mulls security certification for IoT devices
items.summary: The government is considering extending its cybersecurity rules beyond internet-enabled CCTV cameras to a wider range of IoT devices, including smart meters, amid concerns over vulnerabilities in imported products, particularly from China.

items.title: SBI Funds Management IPO opens tomorrow: GMP, issue details, 10 key things to know
items.summary: SBI Funds Management IPO opens for subscription on 14 July, closing on 16 July. The IPO, entirely an Offer for Sale, aims to raise ₹11,102 crore, with existing promoters offloading stakes. It has attracted significant interest from global and Indian institutional investors.

items.title: Global Market: European stocks edge lower as Middle East escalation dampens investor sentiment
items.summary: The pan-European STOXX 600 index slipped 0.3% to 639.29 in early trade, extending losses after posting its biggest weekly decline since late April in the previous session.

items.title: SBI Funds reduces IPO size to Rs 9,813 crore after pre-offer placement. Will it impact listing gains?
items.summary: SBI Funds Management has cut its IPO size to Rs 9,813 crore from Rs 11,693 crore after raising Rs 1,655 crore through a pre-IPO placement with 30 anchor investors. The IPO, entirely an offer-for-sale by SBI and Amundi India Holding, will open on July 14. SBI sold 1.42% stake at Rs 574 per share, the upper price band.

items.title: FIIs cut stakes, but these 10 stocks rallied up to 220% in just over 3 months
items.summary: Despite FII stake reductions in the March quarter, several BSE 500 stocks rallied sharply, with top performers delivering returns of up to 220%, highlighting that foreign selling doesn't always dictate stock performance.

items.title: Swiggy shares slide over 2% after FSSAI issues 9 notices over consumer complaints
items.summary: Swiggy shares fell over 2% on Monday, extending losses for a second session after the Food Safety and Standards Authority of India (FSSAI) issued nine notices to Swiggy Instamart over consumer complaints alleging food safety violations. The regulator has sought a detailed explanation, compliance report and documentary evidence, warning that failure to comply could invite legal action.

items.title: Moneybox picks London’s Pisces market for employee share sale
items.summary: £45mn offering gives further boost to private markets platform after Wayve became first big company to use it

items.title: Europe's Anduril rival Helsing raises $1.8 billion at $18 billion valuation
items.summary: Helsing said "investor demand significantly exceeded the available allocation" for its $1.8 billion funding round.

items.title: Radhika Gupta on Where to Invest Now | Stocks, Gold, International Funds & More
TCS shares rally 8% in 2 days. Time to buy after sharp 32% crash in 2026? Here’s what technical charts indicate
items.summary: TCS shares have surged 8% over the past two sessions after the IT major reported strong Q1 earnings and announced a multi-million-dollar AI-led network transformation deal with ABB. While technical indicators point to improving momentum, analysts remain divided on whether the recent rally marks the beginning of a sustained trend reversal.

items.title: DMart Q1: Speedbump or structural slowdown?
items.summary: Avenue Supermarts, operating DMart, reports a Q1FY27 revenue rise of 15.1%, but competition from quick commerce firms affects growth. Mature store productivity declines while non-metro locations show better performance. The company plans to enhance delivery speed and expand its store network.

items.title: IT rally rescues Nifty from gap-down abyss; crude surge keeps bulls on edge
items.summary: Buying at lower levels and improving market breadth helped benchmarks recover most of their early losses despite higher crude prices and geopolitical concerns

items.title: SK Hynix South Korean shares clock worst day, sinking over 15%, after stellar Nasdaq debut
items.summary: SK Hynix Inc. shares tumbled over 15% in Seoul on Monday after the chipmaker's blockbuster Nasdaq debut.

items.title: US Stock Market: Fed Chair Kevin Warsh unveils expert panels to overhaul US Central Bank policy framework
items.summary: Federal Reserve Chairman Kevin Warsh announced a comprehensive review of monetary policy. Five taskforces comprising prominent economists will lead this extensive review. These panels include experts from academia, business, and technology sectors. The review will examine monetary policy, communication, data analysis, and operational decision-making. This broad effort signals a reassessment of key aspects of the central bank's operations.

items.title: TCS, HCL Tech, Infosys, other IT stocks rally up to 6%, Nifty IT surges 4%. Here's why
items.summary: IT stocks led by TCS, HCL Technologies and Infosys rallied sharply on Monday, lifting the Nifty IT index nearly 4% despite weakness in the broader market. The sectoral index climbed to a more-than one-month high and has gained around 6% over the past two sessions.

items.title: Rajnath Singh, Nitin Gadkari to inaugurate ₹4,700 crore 63-km Lucknow-Kanpur NE-6 Expressway
L&T Finance share price rises 4% after Q1 results 2026. Should you buy, sell or hold?
items.summary: L&T shares opened at ₹321.25 today on NSE, same as the previous close on Friday. The stock touched an intraday high of ₹333.50 per share on 13 July.

items.title: Dubai housing market stays resilient amid Middle East tensions; H1 residential deals touch AED 226 billion: ANAROCK
items.summary: Dubai’s residential real estate market showed resilience despite Middle East geopolitical tensions, with housing transactions worth AED 225.7 billion recorded in H1 2026, according to an ANAROCK report. While the Iran conflict triggered a short-term sentiment-driven correction, strong fundamentals, population growth, foreign investor demand and supportive government policies are expected to keep the market on a recovery path.

items.title: TSMC, the world's largest contract chipmaker, reports 68% surge in June revenue
items.summary: Ahead of its second-quarter earnings, TSMC reported its June and first-half revenue for 2026.

items.title: Global Market: Japan's Nikkei closes lower as oil price jump dims corporate outlook
items.summary: Japan's Nikkei share average closed lower as oil prices rose due to renewed Middle East conflict. Investors weighed the corporate outlook amid increasing costs and the start of earnings season. Chip-related stocks dragged the Nikkei lower, influenced by South Korea's benchmark index. Memory maker Kioxia tanked significantly after a high-profile U.S. listing. Bank shares rose, shifting focus to value stocks and capping the Topix's decline.

items.title: FIIs pour over $1 billion into Indian stocks, biggest weekly buying since June 2025
items.summary: Foreign investors bought over $1 billion worth of Indian equities last week, marking their biggest weekly purchase since June 2025. Goldman Sachs expects inflows to continue, supported by a stable rupee and improving earnings outlook. Global funds were net buyers of $1.3 billion through July 9, followed by another $272 million of provisional purchases on Friday.

items.title: Tata Capital enters gold loans with controlling stake in Kerala-based Yogakshemam Loans
items.summary: Tata Group's financial services arm will acquire an 88.6% stake in Yogakshemam Loans, marking its entry into the secured gold loan business as it expands its retail lending franchise.

items.title: Global Market: AI stocks take a breather after blistering rally as investors reassess valuations
items.summary: AI-linked stocks are showing signs of fatigue as investors lock in profits. Semiconductor and memory-chip makers have seen sharp retreats from recent highs. Investors are now demanding stronger earnings growth and clearer profit evidence. A rotation into software and cloud providers is also occurring within technology. Despite this, the long-term outlook for artificial intelligence remains strong.

items.title: SC upholds Centre's mining royalty formula, rejects Kirloskar's challenge
items.summary: The Supreme Court's ruling is expected to provide certainty to the mining sector by preserving the existing royalty framework, and also comes as a major relief to the Centre and mineral-rich states

items.title: Have India’s top hotel rates peaked? Operators say there’s still room, but not everyone agrees
items.summary: Five-star and luxury hotels in India continue to find room for rate increases compared with global prices. While some believe city hotel rates may be nearing a ceiling, resort pricing holds more potential for upward movement.

items.title: Millworks Technologies IPO GMP hints at over 100% listing pop; key details of the SME IPO
items.summary: As per grey market sources, Millworks Technologies IPO GMP on Monday morning was ₹395. This indicated the stock could list at ₹726 on the BSE SME, a premium of 119% over the issue price.

items.title: Vinay Rajani of HDFC Sec suggests PG Electroplast, Puravankara shares to buy in near-term
items.summary: Indian equity markets faced significant selling on July 13, with Sensex and Nifty 50 falling nearly 1%. The market capitalization dropped by ₹2 lakh crore. Bank Nifty remains strong, while broader markets show resilience, indicating continued investment in high-beta segments.

items.title: Alpine Texworld IPO: GMP, price band, lot size, among 10 key things to know ahead of issue opening
items.summary: Alpine Texworld IPO price band at ₹100 to ₹105 per share. At the upper-end of the price band, the company aims to raise ₹126.25 crore from the book-building issue, which comprises an entirely fresh issue of 1.20 crore equity shares.

items.title: HDFC Bank trims workforce by over 3,300 in FY26, says tech will elevate, not replace people
items.summary: India's largest private sector lender, HDFC Bank, reduced its workforce by 3,343 employees during FY26. Managing Director and CEO Sashidhar Jagdishan said in the annual report that employees must adapt as the organisation evolves.

items.title: Just Dial share price skyrockets 17% on strong Q1 results, new CEO, CFO announcement. Do you own?
items.summary: Just Dial share price touched an intraday high of ₹662.50 per share on 13 July.

items.title: Magellanic Cloud arm wins ₹6.93-crore CCTV surveillance contract from South Central Railway
items.summary: The project, to be completed within 12 months, includes AI-enabled video management infrastructure, networking, optical fibre, NVRs and related systems

items.title: Kalyan Jewellers shares surge 12%, jump 50% in just 4 sessions; is there more steam left?
items.summary: Kalyan Jewellers shares hit their 52-week low of ₹327.15 on 11 June this year. At the current juncture, they are up nearly 62% from their 52-week lows. Meanwhile, it is still over 14% down from its 52-week high of ₹617.30, scaled on 24 July last year.

items.title: CNBC Daily Open: Chips ahoy, no Strait answer and earnings on deck
items.summary: Sharp declines for South Korea's SK Hynix spook markets in Asia and weigh on futures for Europe and the U.S.

items.title: The real worry for Indian metal stocks isn't FPI selling. It's more earnings downgrades.
items.summary: June's biggest foreign investor outflow from the sector since August 2023 appears largely tactical, but analysts warn earnings downgrades are likely to continue as cost pressures persist and commodity markets weaken.

items.title: IPO GMPs: Kusumgar IPO vs Laser Power IPO vs SBI Funds Management IPO vs Alpine Texworld IPO - here's what GMP hints
items.summary: Four IPOs—SBI Funds Management, Alpine Texworld, Kusumgar, and Laser Power—will dominate this week’s market, with investors tracking subscription data and allotment statuses ahead of listing dates on July 15 and 21, 2026.

items.title: Kusumgar IPO allotment to be finalised today. Here's GMP, how to check status
items.summary: Kusumgar IPO bidding opened for subscription on July 8 and closed on July 10.

items.title: HCL Tech Q1 Results 2026 LIVE: PAT jumps 20%, revenue rises 14%; board declares ₹12 interim dividend
items.summary: HCL Tech Q1 Results 2026 LIVE: HCL Technologies is expected to report muted earnings growth in the quarter ended June 2026, with revenue and margin seen improving slightly on a sequential basis. Analysts expect HCL Technologies to reiterate its revenue and margin growth guidance for FY27.

items.title: In defence of . . . prediction markets?
items.summary: Turning everything into a wager can get ugly, but has its upsides

items.title: LTM needs faster revenue growth to meet FY27 goal
items.summary: Analysts caution that weak Q1FY27 results have raised the ask-rate for LTM to meet its guidance of higher growth in FY27

items.summary: Plus, Japan pension fund urged to invest at home, investors cut back on Asian chipmakers and Roy Lichtenstein: Like New

items.title: Crude oil futures jump more than 4% as US-Iran conflict escalates
items.summary: At 10.07 am on Monday, September Brent oil futures were $79.16, up 4.14%, while August WTI crude oil futures were $74.43, up 4.23%

items.title: CarTrade Tech share price gains after UBS assigns ‘Buy’ call, sees over 42% upside potential
items.summary: CarTrade Tech share price has risen 19% in one month, and has gained 56% in three months. The stock has rallied 48% in one year, and has delivered multibagger returns of 237% in two years and 443% over the past three years.

items.title: Gold slides over 1% as oil surges on Strait of Hormuz closure fears
items.summary: Spot gold dropped 1.5% ‌to $4,059.11 per ounce

items.title: Stocks to buy or sell: Dharmesh Shah of ICICI Sec suggests buying Shriram Finance, Brigade Enterprise on 13 July
items.summary: Indian benchmark indices fell on Monday as tensions in the Middle East and Iran's claim of closing the Strait of Hormuz dampened sentiment. Nifty 50 declined 0.69%, and BSE Sensex dropped 0.78%, with all sector indices in negative territory amidst rising crude oil prices.

items.title: Rupee feels the heat of escalating West Asia war; opens 39 paise weaker
items.summary: The Indian currency opened at 95.72 per US Dollar against the previous close of 95.33. Currently, it is trading at 95.75

items.title: A dozen stocks, including PSU names SAIL, IREDA, Power Grid, Mazagon Dock and ONGC, to remain in focus on Monday
items.summary: Stocks across infrastructure, energy, defence, pharmaceuticals, hospitality and manufacturing are expected to attract investor attention on Monday following a series of corporate announcements and regulatory filings

items.title: HCL Tech share price edges higher ahead of Q1 results 2026. Should you buy or sell?
items.summary: HCL Technologies share price surged as much as 0.61% to ₹1,171.20 apiece in Monday's trading session ahead of the company's financial results announcement.

items.title: Avenue Supermarts share price slips over 4% after Q1 results. Should you buy, sell or hold DMart shares?
items.summary: Avenue Supermarts reported an 11.3% year-on-year rise in consolidated net profit to ₹860.6 crore in the quarter ended June 2026, while the company’s revenue grew 14.9% YoY to ₹18,794 crore.

items.title: Sensex, Nifty slide at open as Iran strikes rattle sentiment; IT stocks buck trend
items.summary: Against the weak broader trend, information technology stocks emerged as the standout outperformers at the open

items.title: SBI Funds IPO anchor book 20 times subscribed; draws Capital Group, BlackRock, Goldman, ADIA, GIC
items.summary: The public issue will open for subscription on July 14, close on July 16, and shares are expected to trade on July 21

items.summary: Top US banks benefit from revival of large deals as AI optimism and strong consumer fuel market rally

items.summary: TSMC, SK Hynix and Samsung Electronics together account for 29% of MSCI Emerging Markets index

items.summary: KOSPI fell ‌as ⁠much as 6.13% to hit the lowest ⁠since May 20 and trigger a "sidecar" trading curb, temporarily halting algorithm trading

items.title: EU buys record amount of gas from Russia’s flagship plant
items.summary: Europe purchased almost all the LNG produced by the Yamal facility in the first half of 2026 ahead of import ban

items.title: Oil prices jump over 3% as Iran expands Gulf strikes, raising supply fears
items.summary: Brent crude rose above $78 a barrel, while WTI climbed above $73, as shipping traffic through the strategic waterway slowed sharply

items.title: U.S. and Iran trade airstrikes again amid conflicting claims over Strait of Hormuz
items.summary: The U.S. and Iran exchanged a barrage of missile and drone airstrikes amid a dispute over the status of the Strait of Hormuz.

items.title: Nifty may open 200 points lower as Iran tensions hit sentiment
items.summary: Focus will be on results, FPI activity

items.title: An emboldened India holds out for better terms in US trade talks
items.summary: India rejected a swift trade agreement with the United States, seeking more favorable terms. New Delhi's confidence stems from new trading partners and eased economic risks. India's government official stated they will not rush into an unfavorable deal. Rising exports and diversification initiatives have strengthened India's negotiating position. India is calculating potential legal and political setbacks for US trade measures.

items.title: Traders braced for won volatility after blockbuster SK Hynix listing
items.summary: South Korean chipmaker set to repatriate more than $26bn from share sale over the next month

items.title: McConnell provides health update after long unexplained absence; says he suffered fall, pneumonia
items.summary: McConnell is the longest-serving party leader in Senate history.

items.title: CNBC Daily Open: Mideast tensions on the boil again as U.S., Iran trade strikes
items.summary: Renewed tensions take center stage in the Middle East, sending oil prices spiking once again as the U.S. and Iran trade strikes.

items.title: Jindal Steel’s leadership churn continues as CEO quits within a year
items.summary: Rajiv Kumar, the CEO of Vedanta Aluminium Metal, is slated to succeed outgoing CEO Gautam Malhotra. Former Hindustan Zinc CFO Sandeep Modi is likely to join as the new CFO.

items.title: 10 years of Pokémon Go and the millions still trying to catch 'em all
items.summary: Michael Steranka, vice president at the mobile game's owner Scopely, says it has always been about bringing people together.

items.title: New era for Gibraltar with removal of 118-year-old border controls with Spain
items.summary: It is hoped that free movement between the UK territory and Spain will provide an economic boost.

items.summary: Changing your broadband or energy supplier, or even your bank, for a better deal is simpler than it used to be.

items.summary: Traders fear escalating hostilities could further restrict the flow of crude through the Strait of Hormuz

items.title: Sen. Lindsey Graham, influential lawmaker and Trump ally, dies at 71 after a brief illness
items.summary: Graham, a prominent Republican senator from South Carolina, had just returned from a trip to Ukraine.

items.title: Elon Musk and Sam Altman spar on X after Apple files OpenAI lawsuit
items.summary: Sam Altman insisted that Elon Musk was again obsessed with him because of an OpenAI model release earlier this week.

items.title: Private capital should play a much larger role in India's infrastructure development: Rajkiran Rai, MD & CEO, NaBFID
items.summary: India requires infrastructure investments of nearly Rs 770 lakh crore over the next 20 years. This requirement cannot be met through budgetary resources alone; private capital will need to play a larger role, says Rajkiran Rai, managing director and chief executive of National Bank for Financing Infrastructure and Development (NaBFID).

items.title: Decoding the India-UK CETA: Check what's changing in terms of tariffs, import duty & more
items.summary: The India-UK trade agreement, effective July 15, offers Indian exports significant market share growth. This historic deal removes tariffs on many Indian goods entering the United Kingdom. British products like chocolate and auto parts will see reduced import duties in India. Indian companies can now bid on non-sensitive government procurement contracts in the UK. The agreement addresses trade barriers, benefiting sectors like steel and marine products.

items.title: Japan's Sompo eyes big India play after insurance reforms
items.summary: In the wake of an increase in the FDI limit, Japan's Sompo Holdings is keen on expanding its operations in India, viewing it as a crucial market for future endeavors. With aspirations to utilize their global knowledge, Sompo aims to bolster Universal Sompo's growth and potentially venture into various well-being sectors within India, showcasing a steadfast dedication to the Indian market.

items.title: RBI steps up scrutiny of Corporate India's foreign investments
items.summary: Indian banks are now mandated to disclose their anti-money laundering measures concerning international business partners. Corporations with overseas investments will relay this information to the Reserve Bank of India.

items.title: Graham’s death complicates myriad GOP goals in Congress
items.summary: South Carolina Republican Sen. Lindsey Graham died unexpectedly at 71.

items.title: TCS reshuffles top ranks, reorganizes business units amid growth push
items.summary: Tata Consultancy Services Ltd has initiated a major leadership overhaul, shifting around a dozen senior executives to enhance accountability and growth after its first revenue decline since FY26. The company's restructuring includes new business segments and aims to adapt to evolving market demands.

items.title: Five-year-high grain stocks offer India an El Niño buffer
items.summary: As the risk of an El Niño looms over the kharif season, India's largest foodgrain stockpile in five years gives the government a cushion against weather-led supply disruptions. The higher reserves also provide greater flexibility to curb food inflation and meet welfare needs.

items.title: Burnout, frustration and heartbreak: Amazon layoffs take their toll in saturated job market
items.summary: In the eight-plus months since Amazon announced its most expansive job cuts ever, laid off workers have been thrust into an increasingly saturated labor market.

items.title: Majority of U.S. workers support an AI wealth fund as tech layoffs surge, survey finds
items.summary: A majority of U.S. employees now want an AI sovereign wealth fund to hold corporations more accountable, according to a recent survey, as tech layoffs rise.

items.title: 'Tariff repair remains important': Airtel VC says as Sunil Mittal charts next big bets — financial services, data centre
items.summary: Bharti Airtel aims to grow through financial services, data centres, and cloud, having invested over ₹3.3 lakh crore in digital infrastructure. It also reiterated its push for tariff repair. Here's why Airtel says India needs tariff repair and where the company is investing.

items.title: Hero sounds first warning on CAFE's emerging risk to two-wheeler firms
items.summary: Hero MotoCorp becomes the first two-wheeler maker to flag potential business risks from upcoming CAFE norms, which may increase costs. The company aims to counter these effects by enhancing vehicle efficiency and boosting its electric vehicle offerings ahead of the 2028 emissions deadline.

items.title: Former H&M India executives bet on premium fashion boom with Confluxe
items.summary: The new venture plans to bring international apparel brands to India through an asset-light model, targeting the gap between fast fashion and luxury.

items.title: Volkswagen CEO says ‘smarter solutions’ possible beyond closing plants
items.summary: Volkswagen CEO Oliver Blume feels there are “smarter solutions” to save money than shutting factories. In remarks that could help defuse tensions with the manufacturer’s powerful union, he hailed progress already made in cutting costs.

items.title: HDFC Bank top brass get hefty FY26 pay hikes; Deputy MD's ₹17.14 crore pay package tops CEO Sashidhar Jagdishan's
items.summary: HDFC Bank MD and CEO Sashidhar Jagdishan’s basic pay held steady at ₹3.09 crore and allowances totaled ₹3.46 crore, his performance bonus skyrocketed nearly 35 percent

items.title: India's households miss global shift to financial assets: UBS
items.summary: Global personal wealth increased significantly in 2025 due to strong financial markets. India's household wealth, however, remains dominated by property and physical assets. Financial assets constitute only about twenty-six percent of gross wealth in India. This composition differs greatly from many developed economies and China. Indian households also carry relatively modest debt levels compared to other nations.

items.title: Recycling body MRAI urges govt to remove 2.5% duty on aluminium scrap
items.summary: The recycling industry is urging the government to abolish a duty on aluminium scrap. This removal will lower input costs for domestic manufacturers and recyclers. India currently imports most of its aluminium scrap to meet industrial demand. The secondary aluminium sector supports many jobs and contributes to the circular economy. Other major base metal scraps already enjoy duty-free import status in India.

items.title: India, Canada conclude third round of talks on proposed trade pact
items.summary: India and Canada concluded their third CEPA negotiation round in Ottawa. Both nations aim to finalize this comprehensive economic partnership agreement this year. Discussions covered trade in goods, services, and intellectual property rights. Bilateral trade targets fifty billion US dollars by the year 2030. This agreement seeks to strengthen economic ties between the two countries.

items.title: 'Agreed upon new deal that builds on FTA': New Zealand PM hails PM Modi's visit
items.summary: India and New Zealand elevated their bilateral ties to a strategic partnership. Both nations aim to double bilateral trade between them by the year 2030. Prime Minister Christopher Luxon stated this will boost jobs and wages in New Zealand. Extensive discussions covered trade, defense, and cultural exchanges between the leaders. Several Memoranda of Understanding were exchanged following the productive talks.

items.title: India's beverage market is moving beyond fizzy drinks. FMCG companies are racing to catch up
items.summary: Beverage companies in India are shifting gears, launching innovative drinks that cater to the demand for healthier and functional options. As fizzy drinks see a slowdown, companies like Hindustan Unilever and Varun Beverages are tapping into year-round consumption opportunities.

items.title: How Aldi is taking on US supermarkets with its $4 almond butter
items.summary: The German supermarket's $9bn US push targets urban hubs like Manhattan. Can its discount model match Walmart?

items.title: ‘Almost unlimited’: Execs says AI demand remains strong even as enterprises move to ‘valuemaxxing’
items.summary: AI-related chip stocks have been volatile amid a debate over AI demand and spending.

items.title: Big private equity firms pull in more cash as winners take all
items.summary: Shrinking pool of recipients points to coming wave of so-called zombie firms

items.title: US states push to repurpose abandoned oil wells
items.summary: Pennsylvania, Oklahoma and others are finding ways to turn an expensive and polluting problem into a new opportunity

items.title: China cracks down on top ratings for corporate bonds
items.summary: Regulators pressure agencies to limit triple A designations for higher-interest borrowers

items.title: How Onida plans to reclaim its place in Indian homes
items.summary: Once known for its 'Neighbour's envy, owner's pride' tagline, the consumer electronics brand is targeting mass-premium buyers under a new management team.

items.title: Trump threatens to 'decimate' Iran if it tries to kill him, as Treasury sanctions alleged Iranian financier
items.summary: Trump says 1,000 missiles are "locked and loaded" and will hit Iran if it carries out its threats to assassinate the U.S. president.

items.title: AI is entering a new phase — and these are the stocks investors should be watching
items.summary: CNBC's Paulina Likos and Zev Fima discuss Wall Street's shift from AI euphoria toward rationalization and what it means for investors.

items.title: India, New Zealand elevate ties to strategic partnership; launch 2030 roadmap
items.summary: India and New Zealand elevated their bilateral ties to a strategic partnership. They set a target to double annual trade to thirty-five thousand crore rupees. Defense agreements included mutual logistics support and maritime cooperation. A roadmap for 2030 will guide structured implementation of the partnership. Both nations also agreed on a joint working group on counter-terrorism.

items.title: Bill Gates' daughter's startup Phia investigated for claiming commissions from online sales it didn't generate: Report
items.summary: The company has acknowledged a coding issue and says it has already fixed the problem. However, the allegations have reignited debate over affiliate marketing practices and how browser extensions earn commissions from online purchases.

items.title: How AI rebrands fail to deliver a lasting share price boost
items.summary: Most of the groups that pivoted have not sustained their valuation gains, an FT analysis has found
